center.name: 新地岛
center.summary: 新地岛是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联3种鸟类，包括白颊黑雁、粉脚雁、雪鹀，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: | title=Impacts of highly pathogenic avian influenza (HPAI) on a Barnacle Goose Branta leucopsis population wintering on the Solway Firth, UK | journal=Bird Study | date=2025-02-07 | issn=0006-3657 | doi=10.1080/00063657.2024.2443225 | pages=1–12| doi-access=free }} * Breeding on Novaya Zemlya, Dolgy Island, Kanin Peninsula, Yugorsky Peninsula and the Barents Sea coastline in the Russian Arctic, wintering in the Wadden Sea area in southwest Denmark, northwest Germany and the Netherlands

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: brachyrhynchus }} The pink-footed goose (Anser brachyrhynchus) is a goose which breeds in eastern Greenland, Iceland, Svalbard, and recently Novaya Zemlya

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: |date = 2015|journal = Contemporary Problems of Ecology|doi = 10.1134/S1995425515030117|volume=8|issue = 3|pages=309–316| doi-access=free| bibcode=2015CPrEc...8..309R }} This species is found in the high Arctic tundra of North America, Ellesmere Island, Iceland, higher mountains of Scotland, Norway, Russia, North Greenland, Siberia, Novaya Zemlya, and Franz Josef Land. During the winter, this bird migrates to the circumglobal northern temperate zone including the south of Canada, the northern United States, the coasts and plains of northern Europe in Great Britain, the Netherlands, Germany, Poland, Ukraine, and east to central Asia. During the last ice age, the snow bunting was widespread throughout continental Europe.{{cite journal | last1 = Tomek | first1 = T
